title: York Corpus Christi Play
summary: The pinned repository XML is a copy of a 1993 University of Michigan/Oxford Text Archive electronic edition made from Richard Beadle's 1982 edition, with corrections recorded by Gerald Byron Kinneavy in 1986. Beadle's edition principally transcribes British Library Add MS 35290. The separate Sykes copy of the Scriveners' play, documentary Ordo, and collateral York-related material in Huntington HM 1 are modeled from independently inspected sources without asserting unverified source use by Beadle. The XML encodes fifty divisions and deliberately omits front and back matter, illustrations, and editorial notes. Physical, textual, editorial, electronic, and repository layers are not collapsed into one object.
review_notes: The cataloging boundary is exactly the fifty DIV1 units in York.xml: 1, 2, 3A, 3B, 4-22, 22A, 23-46, 46A, and 47.
The Ironmongers' empty register entry and the Funeral of the Virgin, never entered in the Register, are excluded rather than invented as encoded plays.
Play XVI serializes Yk.16, Yk.16G, and Yk.16M shared and alternative blocks; its 539 L elements are not asserted as 539 additive intellectual lines.
Huntington HM 1 is a distinct Towneley compilation preserving York-derived adaptation or borrowing. Its selected digital object contains no exact York target text and is modeled target-work absent.
The exact CME_from_OTA path notice, not the repository root software license, governs the distributed York artifact and assigns CC BY-NC-SA 3.0.
Complete local-copy coverage always refers to the accessed source entity. It never silently implies a complete physical manuscript or unrestricted redistribution.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for York
entities.description: The pinned XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule from the path-specific CME_from_OTA collection.

entities.label: University of Michigan/Oxford Text Archive electronic edition U-1392-B
entities.description: A 1993 electronic edition based on Beadle 1982 and corrected from Kinneavy 1986. The four text files cover the full fifty-division electronic sequence; a fifth file documents input conventions.

entities.label: Richard Beadle, The York Plays (1982)
entities.description: The immediate printed source named in the electronic header. Its contents include the fifty encoded pageant divisions and also modern front matter, notes, appendices, glossary, the unregistered Funeral text, and other editorial matter omitted from the encoding.

bibliographic.citation: The York Plays, ed. Richard Beadle (London: Edward Arnold, 1982), 537 pp.
bibliographic.title: The York Plays

entities.label: Gerald Byron Kinneavy, A Concordance to the York Plays (1986)
entities.description: A concordance whose pp. xxxi-xxxii record corrections to Beadle's edition incorporated in the electronic text.

bibliographic.citation: Gerald Byron Kinneavy, A Concordance to the York Plays, Garland Reference Library of the Humanities 626 (New York: Garland, 1986), xxxviii + 936 pp.
bibliographic.title: A Concordance to the York Plays
bibliographic.series: Garland Reference Library of the Humanities 626

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 35290 (York Register)
entities.description: The principal surviving civic register of the York pageants, copied chiefly c. 1463-1477 and repeatedly revised into the sixteenth century. It contains the pageant texts represented by the encoding, lacunae and blank entries, and later additions; it is not itself the 1993 electronic text.

holding.notes: Current physical consultation is restricted and requires a letter of introduction and advance request.

physical_description.extent: 254 folios plus starred inserts and flyleaves; the facsimile/critical-edition foliation counts 268 parchment leaves including blanks
notes: Current catalog languages are Middle English and Latin.
Pageant text is physically nonsequential at the Purification, later Coronation fragment, and several blank or inserted leaves.

entities.label: British Library current catalog description for Add MS 35290
entities.description: Current official manuscript record with a detailed folio map, physical description, provenance, access status, and catalog-data licensing statement.

entities.label: York City Archives, Acc 104/G.1 (Sykes manuscript)
entities.description: A separate c. 1525-1550 guild copy of the Scriveners' Incredulity of Thomas on four vellum leaves. Cawley's collation finds that neither this copy nor the Register was copied directly from the other; their precise common exemplar remains uncertain.

holding.notes: The exact current item page was unavailable during review; the current shelfmark is established by scholarly and repository references, with access arranged through York City Archives.

physical_description.extent: four leaves in one quire, with vellum cover and flap
notes: Cawley published a photograph of f. 1a, not a complete photographic facsimile.

entities.label: A. C. Cawley, The Sykes MS of the York Scriveners' Play (1952)
entities.description: Article containing a complete transcription and collation of the Sykes Scriveners copy against the Register, with one manuscript photograph.

bibliographic.citation: A. C. Cawley, ‘The Sykes MS of the York Scriveners' Play,’ Leeds Studies in English 7-8 (1952): 45-80.
bibliographic.title: The Sykes MS of the York Scriveners' Play

entities.label: Leeds digital facsimile of Cawley's 1952 article
entities.description: A complete 38-page PDF of the article and cover sheet. It contains the complete diplomatic transcription but only one photograph of the Sykes manuscript.

entities.label: A/Y Memorandum Book containing the Ordo Paginarum
entities.description: A civic documentary manuscript containing the layered Ordo pageant list. It documents pageant organization but is not a script witness of the exact cycle text. The Ordo originated in 1415 and was erased, supplemented, and rewritten; flood damage in 1892 makes early transcripts important.

historical_designations: A/Y Memorandum Book, York City Library
holding.notes: Current REED attribution places the source at the Borthwick Institute, while older scholarship calls it York City Library/York City Archives. The exact present catalog record and shelfmark mapping remain unresolved.

entities.label: Records of Early English Drama York collection
entities.description: A current TEI documentary edition of York civic records, pinned to commit 1bec27e917f17f9ebdefc6ec6f9c79c8a1605f66. It is evidence for performance and pageant organization, not a transcription of the exact York play script.

entities.label: Peter Meredith, The Ordo Paginarum and the Development of the York Tilemakers' Pageant (1980)
entities.description: Scholarly article demonstrating that the Ordo is a layered and revised document and that the Tilemakers entry cannot be treated as a homogeneous 1415 state.

bibliographic.citation: Peter Meredith, ‘The Ordo Paginarum and the Development of the York Tilemakers' Pageant,’ Leeds Studies in English, n.s. 11 (1980): 59-73.
bibliographic.title: The Ordo Paginarum and the Development of the York Tilemakers' Pageant

entities.label: Digital facsimile of Meredith's 1980 Ordo article
entities.description: A complete 15-page article PDF. It contains one Ordo image, not a full manuscript facsimile.

entities.label: Huntington Library, HM 1 (Towneley manuscript)
entities.description: The distinct Towneley manuscript compilation. Selected pageants contain York-tradition borrowing or adaptation, but HM 1 does not contain the exact York target work and is not modeled as its physical source witness.

holding.notes: Research access is restricted and arranged through Huntington curatorial procedures.

entities.label: Huntington public 60-canvas selected-image object for HM 1
entities.description: The complete provider-selected 60-canvas object, partial relative to the 132-folio manuscript. Its York-related Towneley passages are collateral adaptations or borrowings, not the exact York target text.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and 60 JPEG derivatives

entities.label: Lucy Toulmin Smith, York Plays (1885)
entities.description: The first complete printed edition, made directly from the then Ashburnham manuscript. Smith expanded contractions, regularized selected characters, supplied titles, dramatis personae, analysis, stage directions, scenes, and stanza numbering, and collated the Sykes and Towneley materials.

bibliographic.citation: York Plays: The Plays Performed by the Crafts or Mysteries of York on the Day of Corpus Christi in the 14th, 15th, and 16th Centuries, ed. Lucy Toulmin Smith (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1885).
bibliographic.title: York Plays

entities.label: Internet Archive/Google scan of Smith's 1885 York Plays
entities.description: A complete 667-page PDF scan with complete OCR, scan-data XML, and item metadata cached for verification.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, scan-data XML, and item metadata

entities.label: Internet Archive restricted preview of Beadle's 1982 York Plays
entities.description: A controlled-lending item from which the title, copyright, and two contents pages were publicly previewed and cached as OCR. It is not a complete public facsimile.

physical_description.extent: Item metadata plus four preview-page OCR objects
physical_description.format: Restricted printed-volume scan with public preview

entities.label: The York Play facsimile of the York Register and Ordo (1983)
entities.description: A print facsimile reproducing the surviving York Register and an Ordo section, with modern introductory matter. No complete public digital copy was found.

bibliographic.citation: Richard Beadle, Peter Meredith, and Richard Rastall, The York Play: A Facsimile of British Library MS Additional 35290, Together with a Facsimile of the Ordo Paginarum, Leeds Texts and Monographs, Medieval Drama Facsimiles 7 (Leeds, 1983), lxi + [510] plates.
bibliographic.title: The York Play
bibliographic.series: Leeds Texts and Monographs, Medieval Drama Facsimiles 7

entities.label: Richard Beadle, The York Plays, revised EETS edition (2009-2013)
entities.description: A substantially revised and expanded two-volume edition of the 1982 work. It is later scholarship, not the source of the 1993 electronic encoding.

bibliographic.citation: The York Plays, ed. Richard Beadle, 2 vols., Early English Text Society, Supplementary Series 23-24 (Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2009-2013).
bibliographic.title: The York Plays
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Supplementary Series 23-24

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A: Duration of Copyright (April 2026)
entities.description: Official legal guidance consulted only for the United States term status of Smith's 1885 edition and its mechanical scan. It does not decide provider terms, foreign jurisdictions, manuscript-image rights, or modern editorial layers.

rights.component: Underlying anonymous medieval and early-modern York dramatic texts
rights.basis: The anonymous dramatic texts and the surviving Register long predate modern copyright terms; this statement does not cover modern editions, markup, catalog prose, or manuscript photography.

rights.component: Pinned York XML text, data, and markup distributed in CME_from_OTA
rights.basis: The exact CME_from_OTA folder notice says the texts derive from OTA files and redistributes them under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0. The repository root software license is not substituted for this path-specific notice.
notes: The license record concerns the distributed repository artifact; the underlying historical text and upstream electronic/provider layers are assessed separately.

rights.component: Current University of Michigan digital item and electronic text
rights.basis: The current University of Michigan item states that the materials are in the public domain and supplies the No Copyright - United States rights statement.
notes: The current U-M assertion differs from the OTA Academic Use terms and from the path-specific downstream CME license; those layers are retained separately.

rights.component: Oxford Text Archive U-1392-B text files and delivery
rights.basis: The current OTA record labels the item Academic Use and its terms require attribution, noncommercial use, and permission for uses outside the stated license. Public download availability does not remove those provider terms.

rights.component: Beadle's 1982 edition, editorial matter, and restricted digital preview
rights.basis: The inspected copyright page states copyright 1982 Richard Beadle, reserves all rights, and requires prior publisher permission for reproduction; no open license was identified.

rights.component: Internet Archive controlled-lending scan and public preview OCR of Beadle 1982
rights.basis: The digital surrogate reproduces a copyrighted 1982 edition and is exposed only through controlled lending and limited public preview; no open license was identified.

rights.component: British Library catalog descriptive text and data
rights.basis: The current catalog record expressly licenses catalog content under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0; that grant does not cover manuscript images or third-party content.

rights.component: Modern British Library manuscript images and reproductions
rights.basis: No public image sequence or image-specific open license was available. The British Library directs reproduction requests through Imaging Services and separate permissions procedures.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Scriveners' pageant text
rights.basis: The separate guild copy and underlying anonymous pageant text date to the sixteenth century; physical access and reproduction conditions remain distinct.

rights.component: Cawley's 1952 article, transcription, apparatus, and manuscript photograph
rights.basis: The complete article is publicly downloadable, but no item-specific open license or definitive United States term determination was identified; the medieval underlying text is assessed separately.

rights.component: Leeds digital article scan and delivery file
rights.basis: The scan reproduces the potentially protected 1952 article and its manuscript photograph; no independent open license for the delivery file was identified.

rights.component: Underlying medieval and early-modern Ordo documentary text
rights.basis: The historical documentary text long predates modern copyright terms; archive copy-service and image conditions remain separate.

rights.component: REED York TEI XML, editorial transcription, and repository data
rights.basis: The pinned REED Collections repository license applies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 to the collection.

rights.component: Meredith's 1980 article and its Ordo image
rights.basis: A complete article scan is publicly available, but no item-specific open license or definitive term determination was identified.

rights.component: Digital article scan and delivery file
rights.basis: The scan reproduces the potentially protected 1980 article and its Ordo image; no independent open license for the delivery file was identified.

rights.component: Underlying medieval and early-modern Towneley compilation texts
rights.basis: The manuscript texts long predate modern copyright terms. This statement does not cover Huntington's modern photography or provider terms, which are recorded on the selected facsimile entity.

rights.component: Huntington selected manuscript images, IIIF manifest, and delivery
rights.basis: The exact object supplies no explicit open license and refers users to Huntington's rights policy, which places rights determinations on the researcher and distinguishes physical ownership from copyright.
notes: The cached object is collateral Towneley material and contains no exact York target text.

rights.component: Smith's 1885 edition text and editorial matter
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1885, before the current United States public-domain cutoff described in U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A.

rights.component: Internet Archive/Google mechanical scan, OCR, and delivery files for Smith 1885
rights.basis: No separately licensed creative scan layer is identified for the public-domain volume; provider service terms remain applicable.

rights.component: Kinneavy's 1986 concordance and correction list
rights.basis: The modern publication remains within ordinary copyright terms and no open license was identified.

rights.component: 1983 facsimile volume, introduction, and photographic plates
rights.basis: The modern facsimile and editorial matter were published in 1983; no open license or complete public digital copy was identified.

rights.component: Revised 2009-2013 EETS edition and editorial matter
rights.basis: The modern two-volume edition remains within ordinary copyright terms and no open license was identified.

rights.component: Official Circular 15A text and PDF delivery file
rights.basis: The circular is an official U.S. Copyright Office publication and publicly downloadable, but this manifest does not make a separate statutory determination for its delivery file.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Beadle's complete 537-page printed edition, including illustrations, front and back matter, editorial notes, and pageant texts
editorial_practices.representation: Fifty electronic pageant divisions; illustrations, front and back matter, and editorial notes omitted
editorial_practices.description: The electronic header expressly defines a selective representation of the printed edition rather than a complete page facsimile or transcription of every editorial layer.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Separate Masons' Herod and Goldsmiths' Magi pageant units in the Register, with a shared middle scene preserved in alternative versions
editorial_practices.representation: One editorial Play XVI contents container, Masons / Goldsmiths — Herod / Magi, spanning printed pp. 135-149
editorial_practices.description: Beadle's contents combine two intellectual pageant units in one numbered editorial container. The work manifest therefore counts two nested components while retaining the fifty-container electronic structure.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Yk.16 shared outer material plus alternative Yk.16M and Yk.16G middle blocks in the OTA source files
editorial_practices.representation: A single XML DIV1 N=16 containing 539 L elements serialized from shared and alternative blocks
editorial_practices.description: The electronic serialization preserves both alternative branches within one container; its raw L-element count is a representation metric, not a claim that all branches form one additive intellectual line sequence.

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded contractions and selected regularized characters in the printed edition
editorial_practices.description: Smith explicitly describes expansion and character treatment in the edition's introductory statement; this modern editorial layer is not attributed to the manuscript.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Register text, divisions, speaker cues, and stage material without Smith's complete modern navigational apparatus
editorial_practices.representation: Supplied play titles, dramatis personae, analyses, scenes, stage directions, and stanza numbering
editorial_practices.description: Smith distinguishes her supplied editorial apparatus from the manuscript-derived text; later users must not treat those additions as physical-witness features.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The separate four-leaf Sykes Scriveners copy and the corresponding Register text
editorial_practices.representation: Complete Sykes transcription with Register variants and a textual-relationship analysis
editorial_practices.description: Cawley's collation demonstrates that neither surviving copy is a direct transcript of the other; it does not authorize collapsing them into one witness.

evidence.citation: Targeted structural inspection of the pinned York.xml and its exact OTA source files.
evidence.summary: Establishes the exact electronic boundary and raw representation counts. It also confirms that Play XVI serializes shared and alternative Yk.16, Yk.16M, and Yk.16G blocks, so raw L counts cannot be treated as additive intellectual lineation.

evidence.summary: The path-specific notice says the CME_from_OTA texts derive from OTA files and are redistributed under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0. It is the relevant repository-artifact grant and is not replaced by the root software license.

evidence.citation: Clifford Davidson, ed., The York Corpus Christi Play, Middle English Texts Series reading edition (2011; current web edition).
evidence.summary: Supports the established work title; civic-cycle model; pageant wagons and stations; Register copying and revision; Ordo layers; omitted entries; separate guild copies; Towneley relationships; and performance history through 1569.

evidence.citation: British Library Add MS 35290 access conditions.
evidence.summary: Records restricted consultation, advance request requirements, and the letter-of-introduction condition for the physical manuscript.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services and Imaging Services terms.
evidence.summary: Documents the current route for requesting reference or publication-quality images and the separation of image supply from permissions and licensing.

evidence.citation: A. C. Cawley, ‘The Sykes MS of the York Scriveners' Play,’ Leeds Studies in English 7-8 (1952): 45-80, complete article scan.
evidence.locator: complete article; manuscript description; photograph of f. 1a; diplomatic transcription; Register collation; textual-relationship conclusion
evidence.summary: Establishes the separate four-leaf Scriveners witness, its c. 1525-1550 date, complete transcription and collation, and the conclusion that neither Sykes nor Register copy derives directly from the other.

evidence.citation: York City Archives, Visiting Us.
evidence.summary: Provides current appointment, preordering, and contact procedures for consulting York City Archives holdings; no exact public Acc 104/G.1 item page was located.

evidence.citation: Records of Early English Drama, York collection TEI at pinned commit 1bec27e917f17f9ebdefc6ec6f9c79c8a1605f66.
evidence.locator: complete York civic-record TEI; Ordo and performance records
evidence.summary: Supplies documentary evidence for York's civic performance tradition and Ordo organization. It is a complete source for the accessed REED catalog entity, not a transcription of the exact dramatic target work.

evidence.citation: REEDProject/Collections repository license at commit 1bec27e917f17f9ebdefc6ec6f9c79c8a1605f66.
evidence.summary: Applies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 to the pinned REED collection.

evidence.citation: GitHub commit metadata for REEDProject/Collections commit 1bec27e917f17f9ebdefc6ec6f9c79c8a1605f66.
evidence.summary: Pins the REED XML and license evidence to an exact repository state and commit date.

evidence.citation: Peter Meredith, ‘The Ordo Paginarum and the Development of the York Tilemakers' Pageant,’ Leeds Studies in English, n.s. 11 (1980): 59-73, complete article scan.
evidence.locator: complete article pp. 59-73; Ordo image and layered-entry analysis
evidence.summary: Shows that the Ordo is an erased, supplemented, and rewritten documentary witness rather than a homogeneous 1415 script, and documents flood damage and the value of early transcripts.

evidence.citation: Borthwick Institute for Archives, Visiting Us and document-copy services.
evidence.summary: Provides current physical consultation and copy-request procedures; the exact mapping between the current REED reference and older York City Library/York City Archives designations remains unresolved.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, Towneley cycle: manuscript, mssHM 1.
evidence.summary: Confirms HM 1 as a distinct 132-folio parchment compilation dated 1490-1510 by the current catalog, restricted physical access, the 60-view partially scanned public object, and York-related collateral material without making it the exact York witness.

evidence.citation: Huntington Digital Library IIIF Presentation manifest for p15150coll7:52988 and complete cached 60-canvas selected bundle.
evidence.locator: manifest.json; inventory; all 60 JPEG derivatives at requested 1600-pixel width
evidence.summary: The bundle is complete for the provider-selected digital object and partial for physical HM 1. Inventory and ZIP validation confirm all 60 canvases; the exact York target work is absent.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of selected HM 1 canvases in the cached Huntington bundle.
evidence.locator: Pharaoh f. 21r; Harrowing f. 97v; Resurrection boundary f. 101r; Judgment ff. 127r-v
evidence.summary: Confirms that the selected object displays Towneley versions of York-related material. These are collateral adaptations or borrowings, not the exact York target text, so target_work_presence is absent and no work_portion is asserted.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, Library Rights and Permissions.
evidence.summary: Distinguishes ownership of physical objects from intellectual-property rights, places rights determination on the researcher, does not require Huntington permission for public-domain material, and requests appropriate citation.

evidence.citation: York Plays, ed. Lucy Toulmin Smith (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1885), complete Internet Archive scan and OCR.
evidence.locator: preface and editorial statement; contents; printed pp. 115-129 notes on Masons/Goldsmiths; pp. 448 and 455 Sykes collation; Towneley parallel notes including the Doctors pageant
evidence.summary: Confirms the first complete edition's direct use of the Ashburnham Register, Smith's editorial transformations, separate Masons XVI and Goldsmiths XVII units with shared middle scene and distinct outer material, explicit Sykes collation, and explicit printed Towneley comparisons.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for yorkplaysnowfir00smitgoog.
evidence.summary: Confirms the Internet Archive item identity, public delivery, complete derivative inventory, and 667-page scan record.
notes: The complete cached OCR has SHA-256 70851ac4f6b9521257d83240ce1466d46b098f57e6e53edc079945849612cc5e; scan-data XML has SHA-256 3d2125877033a128bce863d328071c63cb3535d029f99abc3d6ee6e4a57a51f7.

evidence.citation: Library of Congress LCCN 84221144, The York Play: a Facsimile of British Library MS Additional 35290, together with a Facsimile of the Ordo Paginarum (1983).
evidence.summary: Confirms the print facsimile's title, responsibilities, publication date, series, extent, and dual Register/Ordo scope; no complete public digital copy was found.

evidence.citation: Review and bibliographic record for Richard Beadle's revised two-volume EETS York Plays (2009-2013).
evidence.summary: Confirms the later two-volume revised edition's identity and dates. It is later scholarship and not the source of the 1993 electronic encoding.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A: Duration of Copyright (April 2026).
evidence.locator: complete official circular; works first published before 1930
evidence.summary: Supplies current official United States term guidance used narrowly for the 1885 Smith edition and underlying historical texts; it does not decide foreign jurisdictions, provider terms, or modern image/editorial layers.

open_questions.question: What exact textual genealogy and editorial rationale underlie the Yk.16, Yk.16M, and Yk.16G shared and alternative blocks serialized inside Play XVI?
next_steps: Consult the complete 1982 edition and Kinneavy's full correction list, then collate the Register folios, OTA records, and XML serialization without treating alternative branches as additive intellectual lines.
notes: The fifty-container electronic structure and the two nested Masons/Goldsmiths pageant units are secure; the unresolved issue is internal branch genealogy.

open_questions.question: Did Beadle's 1982 edition directly consult or collate the Sykes manuscript or Huntington HM 1?
next_steps: Inspect the complete 1982 introduction, textual notes, and apparatus before adding any Beadle-to-Sykes or Beadle-to-HM 1 source-use relation.
notes: The currently accessible Beadle title, copyright, and contents previews do not prove either relation. Smith's explicit Sykes and Towneley comparisons are modeled separately.

open_questions.question: Which exact electronic passages were changed from Beadle 1982 using Kinneavy's correction list on pp. xxxi-xxxii?
next_steps: Inspect a physical or licensed complete copy of Kinneavy 1986 and record each corrected locus against the OTA files and XML.

open_questions.question: What is the exact current catalog record and shelfmark mapping for the A/Y Memorandum Book containing the Ordo Paginarum?
next_steps: Ask the Borthwick Institute and York City Archives to reconcile REED's current E 20:1327-1547 attribution with older York City Library and York City Archives designations.

open_questions.question: Does a complete current public digital facsimile of British Library Add MS 35290 exist?
next_steps: Periodically recheck the exact British Library record and digitized-manuscript services; meanwhile use the 1983 print facsimile or request reference images from Imaging Services.
notes: The current catalog reports no available digital images despite historical references to full coverage.

open_questions.question: What lost exemplar or transmission path best explains the Sykes and Register versions of the Scriveners' pageant?
next_steps: Preserve Cawley's negative conclusion that neither surviving witness directly copies the other; undertake renewed stemmatic and paleographical analysis before asserting a more specific genealogy.

open_questions.question: How should the current U-M NoC-US assertion, OTA Academic Use terms, and downstream CME_from_OTA CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 grant be reconciled?
next_steps: Seek a provider or repository statement explaining the chain of grants; until then retain each electronic and distribution layer separately and follow the exact path-specific license for the repository artifact.
